#define R_OK 4

Definition at line 38 of file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

Function Documentation

void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t * server)

Wake the WebSocket server event loop immediately. Thread-safe. Wakes lws_service() from its timeout wait, allowing the event loop to check the running flag without waiting for the full service timeout (50ms).

Parameters

server Server instance (must have a valid context)

Definition at line 974 of file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

974                                                                  {
975   if (server && server->context) {
976     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
977   }
978 }




References websocket_server::context.


Referenced by session_server_like_run().






void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Destroy WebSocket server and free resources.


Parameters


server Server instance to destroy


Definition at line 980 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


980                                                           {
981   if (!server) {
982     return;
983   }
984 
985   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
986 
987   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
988   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
989   if (server->context) {
990     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
991     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
992   }
993 
994   // Unregister atomic fields
995   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
996 
997   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
998   if (server->handler_pool) {
999     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
1000     server->handler_pool = NULL;
1001   }
1002 
1003   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
1004   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
1005   // or didn't complete normally.
1006   if (server->context) {
1007     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
1008     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
1009     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
1010     server->context = NULL;
1011   }
1012 
1013   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
1014 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, NAMED_UNREGISTER, websocket_server::running,
    and thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by session_server_like_run().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init
  (websocket_server_t * server, const websocket_server_config_t *
  config)


Initialize WebSocket server. Creates libwebsockets context and
    prepares for accepting connections.




Parameters


server Server instance to initialize


Config Module Server configuration


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 812 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


812                                                                                                              {
813   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
814     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
815   }
816 
817   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
818   server->handler = config->client_handler;
819   server->user_data = config->user_data;
820   server->port = config->port;
821   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
822 
823   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
824   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
825   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
826 
827   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
828   lws_log_init_server();
829 
830   // HTTP mount for /health endpoint (required for LWS_CALLBACK_HTTP to fire)
831   static const struct lws_http_mount health_mount = {
832       .mount_next = NULL,
833       .mountpoint = "/",
834       .mountpoint_len = 1,
835       .origin = "http",
836       .def = NULL,
837       .protocol = "http",
838       .origin_protocol = LWSMPRO_CALLBACK,
839   };
840 
841   // Configure libwebsockets context
842   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
843   info.port = config->port;
844   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
845   info.mounts = &health_mount;
846   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
847   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
848   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
849   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
850   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
851 
852   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
853   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
854       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
855 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
856     // Validate certificate and key files are readable
857     if (platform_access(config->tls_cert_path, R_OK) != 0) {
858       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS certificate file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_cert_path,
859                        errno);
860     }
861     if (platform_access(config->tls_key_path, R_OK) != 0) {
862       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS key file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_key_path, errno);
863     }
864 
865     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
866     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
867     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
868 #else
869     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
870 #endif
871   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
872              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
873     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
874   }
875 
876   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
877   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
878   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
879   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
880 
881   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
882   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
883   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
884   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
885   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
886   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
887 
888   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
889   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
890   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
891   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
892 
893   // Create libwebsockets context
894   // Log diagnostic info before attempting creation
895   log_debug("lws_create_context: port=%u, protocols=%p, options=0x%x", info.port, (void *)info.protocols, info.options);
896 
897   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
898   if (!server->context) {
899     // Capture any OpenSSL errors that may have occurred during context creation
900     unsigned long ssl_err = 0;
901     char ssl_err_str[256] = "no OpenSSL errors in queue";
902 
903     // Extract all errors from OpenSSL error queue
904     while ((ssl_err = ERR_get_error()) != 0) {
905       ERR_error_string_n(ssl_err, ssl_err_str, sizeof(ssl_err_str));
906       log_error("OpenSSL error: %lu: %s", ssl_err, ssl_err_str);
907     }
908 
909     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context (last OpenSSL error: %s)",
910                      ssl_err_str);
911   }
912 
913   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
914   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
915   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
916   if (!server->handler_pool) {
917     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
918     server->context = NULL;
919     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
920   }
921 
922   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
923   char ws_port_name[32];
924   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
925   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
926 
927   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
928   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
929 
930   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
931   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
932 
933   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
934   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
935 }

    websocket_server_config_t::client_handler,

    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    lws_log_init_server(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET,

    thread_pool_create_with_workers(),

    websocket_server_config_t::user_data.


Referenced by session_server_like_run().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run
  (websocket_server_t * server)


Run WebSocket server event loop. Blocks until server is signaled
    to stop via running flag.




Parameters


server Server instance


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 937 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


937                                                                    {
938   if (!server || !server->context) {
939     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
940   }
941 
942   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
943 
944   // Run libwebsockets event loop
945   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
946   int service_call_count = 0;
947   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
948     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
949     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
950     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
951     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
952     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
953     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
954     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
955       // > 30ms gap between service calls
956       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
957       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
958     }
959     service_call_count++;
960     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
961                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
962     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
963     if (result < 0) {
964       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
965       break;
966     }
967   }
968 
969   log_info(
970       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
971   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
972 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_debug_every, log_error, log_info,
    log_info_every, NAMED_DESCRIBE, websocket_server::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO, time_get_ns(),
    and US_PER_MS_INT.
